## §27 UDT-EB-class factor closed-form for NUMBER-COUNT observable (Session 73 S73-002 substantive PONDER + S73-003 five-walk verifier round-trip 2026-05-18)

### §27.1 Statement

At canonical UDT first-order linear in $\delta\phi_1$ on canonical Branch-C §1.4.2 N=2 regular-origin scaffold, the UDT-EB-class factor at the NUMBER-COUNT observable is given by the closed-form

$$\boxed{F_{\rm UDT-EB}^{(p)} \;=\; \frac{|c_{\rm NC}^{(p)}|}{|c_{\rm LOS}^{(p)}|} \;=\; \frac{2 + x(1+\alpha)}{3}}$$

where $\alpha$ is the population-specific spectral index ($L_\nu \propto \nu^{-\alpha}$) and $x = -d\log N(>S)/d\log S$ is the count-slope at flux threshold. The formula is **zero-free-parameter** at canonical UDT content; canonical inputs are $(\alpha, x)$ per population (catalog parameters) plus canonical Method A pinning $\delta_1 = \rho_1/\bar\rho_m = 6.2\%$ per archived VR §84.4 post-§229.16 path (a).

**Status:** DERIVED at canonical content first-order linear (S73-002 substantive PONDER); CONFIRMED at machine precision via five methodology-distinct verifier walks (S73-003 verifier round-trip; AGREE-WITH-SUBSTANTIVE-SHARPENING with CONVERGENT MULTI-VERIFIER CATCH at #29 cascade-confirmation discipline at canonical-content named-component-attribution layer; SE-α-3 + SE-β-1).

### §27.2 Four-component canonical-content derivation (post-S73-003 corrected attribution)

The NUMBER-COUNT observable at canonical UDT decomposes into four structurally distinct canonical-content components at first-order linear in $\delta\phi_1$. Per archived VR §92.5.2 line 10307 verbatim, the canonical-content derivation chain is "kaleidoscope-refraction action on apparent number density × luminosity-function-weighted redshift response × aberration corrections." S70-002 STRUCTURAL-FORM derivation expanded this to four components by separating flux-limit modulation from K-correction. S73-002 substantive PONDER derived the closed-form formula; S73-003 verifier round-trip corrected the named-component attribution.

| Component | Coefficient | Canonical-content origin |
|---|---|---|
| (a) Kaleidoscope-refraction on apparent number density | $c_{\rm K-ref} = -3$ | Lorentz-invariant specific intensity transformation $I_\nu / \nu^3$ at canonical UDT redshift $1+z = e^\phi$ (CG §1.3); three powers of $(1+z)^{-1} = e^{-\phi}$ inherited from $\nu^3$ Lorentz invariance. Sibling to CMB-T LOS measure $e^{-3\phi_0}$ per archived VR §229.3 step 3 + §80.2. |
| (b) LFS / proper-volume Jacobian | $c_{\rm LFS} = +1$ | Canonical spatial-metric proper-volume element $dV = e^{\phi} r^2 \sin\theta \, dr \, d\Omega$ from spatial slice of canonical $ds^2$; first-order linear coefficient $\partial \ln(e^\phi r^2) / \partial \phi = +1$. |
| (c) Aberration — solid-angle redirection at flux-threshold-modulation level | $c_{\rm aber} = -x$ | Canonical-UDT analog of the LCDM EB-1984 "$x \cdot 1$" piece in $x(1+\alpha)$ — flux-threshold modulation by canonical solid-angle redirection under directional $\delta\phi_1$. Distinct from LCDM Lorentz-aberration $+2$ (no boost frame at canonical UDT); enters at first-order linear via canonical kaleidoscope geometry on flux-threshold counts. |
| (d) Flux-limit modulation + K-correction | $c_{\rm flux} = -x\alpha$ | **★ Metric-radiance comparison branch convention (per archived VR §110.3 + §110.5 labeling discipline; Trimble's choice at S76-004 close per F-cand-S76-003-Vε-a substantive sub-finding):** $d_L = (1+z)^2 d_A = r e^{2\phi}$ with $d_A = r$ (exact canonical UDT areal/angular-diameter per archived VR §110.2). **Two-factor convention is a *conditional* measurement map**, not derivation-grounded at canonical-content scope — labeled per archived VR §110.5 forbidden overclaim #3. Threshold $L_{\min}(r, S) = 4\pi r^2 e^{4\phi} S \cdot e^{(\alpha-1)\phi}$; LF power-law $\Phi(>L_{\min}) \propto L_{\min}^{-x}$ gives raw coefficient $-x(3+\alpha)$; canonical-UDT LOS-normalization **additive** correction $+3x$ at zeroth-order produces net $-x\alpha$ (K-correction at canonical UDT alone; flux-threshold-aberration moved to component (c)). **Operationally:** $c_{\rm flux} = \text{raw} + \text{correction} = -x(3+\alpha) + 3x = -x\alpha$; the $+3x$ is an additive correction that removes the kaleidoscope-refraction redundancy at $-3x$ already counted in component (a) $c_{\rm K-ref} = -3$ at the $I_\nu/\nu^3$ specific-intensity layer, isolating the K-correction-alone contribution (per S75-003 Vα-1(d) sub-finding sharpening SE-Vα-1; reader-interpretation-ambiguity prevention). **★ Sum-invariance pin (per S76-004 substantive PONDER):** Component (d) net coefficient $c_{\rm flux} = -x\alpha$ is **invariant under $d_L$-convention choice** at canonical UDT first-order linear in $\delta\phi_1$. Under one-factor convention $d_L = re^\phi$ (derivation-grounded via photon-flux-area at canonical UDT static-universe per Vε S76-003 derivation walk + CG §28 + archived VR §34.9 SNe canonical-record reproduction), raw is $-x(\alpha+1)$ and additive correction is $+x$ (removing kaleidoscope-refraction redundancy at $-x$ scale; smaller correction than two-factor due to fewer redshift factors in $d_L^2$). Net coefficient is the same $-x\alpha$. See new CG §27.12 for full sum-invariance derivation. |
| **Sum** | $c_{\rm NC}^{(p)} = -2 - x(1+\alpha)$ | Four-component total at canonical content first-order linear. |
| **LOS observable (CMB-T-like)** | $c_{\rm LOS}^{(p)} = -3$ | LOS optical-depth observable picks up component (a) only (kaleidoscope-refraction); other components are NC-specific. Sibling to archived VR §229.3 step 3 + §80.2 frame-relation derivation. |

**Ratio (UDT-EB-class factor):**

$$F_{\rm UDT-EB}^{(p)} = \frac{|c_{\rm NC}^{(p)}|}{|c_{\rm LOS}^{(p)}|} = \frac{2 + x(1+\alpha)}{3}$$

### §27.3 Numerical evaluation at canonical population parameters

Canonical population parameters per S70-004b + active doc §3.4:

| Population | $\alpha$ | $x$ | $D_{\rm obs}$ | $F_{\rm UDT-EB}^{(p)}$ canonical | $F_{\rm UDT-EB}^{(p)}$ required | Gap factor |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| CatWISE (Secrest+ 2021) | 1.26 | 1.43 | $1.55 \times 10^{-2}$ | **1.7439** | 12.500 | 7.17× |
| NVSS (Singal 2011 / Rubart-Schwarz 2013) | 0.75 | 1.00 | $1.80 \times 10^{-2}$ | **1.2500** | 14.516 | 11.61× |

Canonical inputs: $\delta_1 = 0.062$ (Method A per archived VR §84.4 post-§229.16 path (a)); $\zeta^{(p,{\rm LOS})} \approx 0.020$ depth-saturated near-uniform per archived VR §92.5.2 deep-population cluster.

**Three independent numerical methodologies converge at machine precision** (diff $0 \times 10^0$): S73-002 phase2_numeric.py torch GPU (original attribution); S73-003 verifier_sympy.py sympy exact (symbolic derivation); S73-003 verifier_numeric.py numpy CPU (corrected attribution).

### §27.4 Second-order $\delta\phi_1^2$ sub-leading bound

Taylor expansion of canonical UDT nonlinear integrand $e^{C\phi}$ around $\phi_0$ with $C = c_{\rm NC} = -2 - x(1+\alpha)$:

$$\zeta^{(2)} = \frac{C^2}{2}, \qquad \frac{|\zeta^{(2)} \delta_1^2|}{|\zeta^{(1)} \delta_1|} = \frac{|C| \cdot \delta_1}{2}$$

At canonical Method A $\delta_1 = 0.062$:

| Population | $|\zeta^{(2)}/\zeta^{(1)}|$ at $\delta_1$ | $|\zeta^{(3)}/\zeta^{(1)}|$ at $\delta_1$ |
|---|---|---|
| CatWISE | 16.22% | 1.75% |
| NVSS | 11.62% | 0.90% |

Taylor series convergent and well-bounded at canonical Method A pinning. Second-order $\delta\phi_1^2$ contribution **cannot supply factor-of-7-to-12× elevation** from canonical $\sim 1.25$–$1.74$ to required $\sim 12$–$14.5$ range.

### §27.5 LCDM Ellis-Baldwin convention comparison

LCDM EB Singal 2011 convention is the canonical pinned convention per S70-005 standing prohibition #4: $D_{\rm kin}^{\rm Singal} = [(1+\alpha) + x] \beta_{\rm kin}$ with $\beta_{\rm kin} = 0.0012$ (solar-frame velocity / c).

| Population | $D_{\rm kin}^{\rm Singal}$ | Observed/Singal multiplier |
|---|---|---|
| CatWISE | $4.428 \times 10^{-3}$ | 3.50× (canonical 4.9σ tension) |
| NVSS | $3.300 \times 10^{-3}$ | 5.45× |

Coefficient sign-reversal between UDT and LCDM reflects small-parameter difference (UDT $\delta_1$ matter-heterogeneity vs LCDM $\beta_{\rm kin}$ velocity-boost). Not the same observable mechanism; same algebraic structure.

### §27.10 BAO galaxy auto-correlation AMPLITUDE sub-scope — first canonical-content derivation of §27.7 signpost-candidate (a) (S75-002 Phase B, 2026-05-18)

**Statement.** At canonical UDT first-order linear in $\delta\phi_1$ in the small-pair-separation regime ($|\Delta r| \ll$ cosmological-dipole-mode-wavelength), the UDT-EB-class factor at the BAO galaxy auto-correlation AMPLITUDE sub-scope (directional dipole modulation of the correlation function magnitude at fixed pair separation, equivalent to the directional dipole of the observed pair-count-rate amplitude at the BAO peak) is:

$$\boxed{F_{\rm UDT-EB}^{(BAO\text{-}amp)} \;=\; \frac{2[2 + x(1+\alpha)]}{3} \;=\; 2 \cdot F_{\rm UDT-EB}^{(NC)}}$$

DERIVED-AT-CANONICAL-CONTENT-FIRST-ORDER-LINEAR-WITH-SUB-LEADING-CORRECTION-BOUND-VERIFIED per S75-002 Phase B. Zero-free-parameter prediction at canonical UDT; inherits canonical inputs $(\alpha, x)$ per population. The factor of 2 relative to the NUMBER-COUNT-observable $F^{(NC)}$ at §27.1 derives from the pair-counts structure of the correlation function — both pair points feel $\delta\phi_1$ at first-order linear in the small-pair-separation regime, producing twice the single-point modulation.

**Sub-scope discipline.** This closed-form applies ONLY to the AMPLITUDE sub-scope of the BAO observable (directional dipole of the correlation function magnitude at fixed pair separation). The BAO PEAK CHARACTERISTIC LENGTH SCALE (the SCALE sub-scope; the comoving position of the acoustic feature) is **canonical-content extension territory distinct from CG §27 operator transferability** — canonical UDT is STATIC per Theory Rule 5; no LCDM recombination epoch in the expanding sense; the BAO peak does NOT serve as a "standard ruler" at canonical UDT in the LCDM Eisenstein-Hu sense. SCALE sub-scope requires separate Charles tier-gate authorization per S67-001 §V SE-S67-001-2 β + S70-001 standing prohibition.

**Standard-ruler-not-available-at-canonical-UDT framing (load-bearing).** What canonical UDT predicts is **amplitude modulation of the correlation function across the sky under directional $\delta\phi_1$**, NOT distance measurement via a fixed comoving reference scale. Any LCDM-shaped "BAO standard ruler" framing imported into canonical-UDT observational interpretation collides with Theory Rule 5; the structural distinction between standard-ruler observable (LCDM) and amplitude-modulation observable (UDT) is the BAO-class sibling to the dipole-kinematic-vs-geometric distinction (number-count dipole) registered as standing prohibition #6 at observable-class scope.

#### §27.10.1 Component-by-component pair-counts transfer

The four CG §27.2 canonical-content components each transfer cleanly to the BAO AMPLITUDE sub-scope at the field-level number-density modulation per pair point. The number density at each pair point inherits the single-point modulation $n_{\rm obs}/\bar{n} = 1 + c_{\rm NC}^{(p)} \delta\phi_1(\vec{r}) + O(\delta\phi_1^2)$ with $c_{\rm NC}^{(p)} = c_{\rm K-ref} + c_{\rm LFS} + c_{\rm aber} + c_{\rm flux} = -2 - x(1+\alpha)$.

| Component | $c$ coefficient | CG §27.2 canonical-content origin | BAO AMPLITUDE pair-counts transfer |
|---|---|---|---|
| (a) Kaleidoscope-refraction | $c_{\rm K-ref} = -3$ | Lorentz-invariant $I_\nu/\nu^3$ + canonical UDT $1+z = e^\phi$; CG §1.3 + archived VR §229.3 step 3 + §80.2 | Field-level number-density modulation per pair point; both pair points modulate at first-order linear |
| (b) LFS/proper-volume Jacobian | $c_{\rm LFS} = +1$ | Spatial-metric proper-volume element $\sqrt{h} = e^\phi r^2 \sin\theta$; CG §5 | Per-pair-point Jacobian at fixed coordinate separation; separation-scale Jacobian is SCALE-feature out-of-scope |
| (c) Aberration | $c_{\rm aber} = -x$ | Canonical-UDT solid-angle redirection at flux-threshold under directional $\delta\phi_1$; CG §1.4.2 + threshold redirection | Per-pair-point threshold modulation; both pair points share same canonical-UDT redirection |
| (d) Flux/K-correction | $c_{\rm flux} = -x\alpha$ | **Metric-radiance comparison branch convention** (per archived VR §110.3 + §110.5 labeling discipline; sibling to §27.2 component (d) S76-004 Option B re-labeling): $d_L = re^{2\phi}$ + power-law LF threshold-crossing; CG §5. **Sum-invariance pin:** net coefficient $-x\alpha$ invariant under $d_L$-convention choice at canonical UDT first-order linear; see CG §27.12 (S76-004 substantive PONDER + S76-005 verifier round-trip + S76-006 cleanup landing). | Per-pair-point K-correction; both pair points share same canonical-UDT flux-luminosity scaling |

All four components transfer at field-level number-density modulation per pair point; pair-counts structure introduces the factor-of-2 modification (§27.10.2 below).

#### §27.10.2 Factor-of-2 derivation (sympy-symbolic verified)

Pair count rate at separation $\Delta r$:
$$\langle n(\vec{r}_1) n(\vec{r}_2) \rangle = \bar{n}^2 \cdot \big[1 + c_{\rm NC}^{(p)} \delta\phi_1(\vec{r}_1)\big] \big[1 + c_{\rm NC}^{(p)} \delta\phi_1(\vec{r}_2)\big]$$

First-order linear expansion in $\delta\phi_1$:
$$\langle n(\vec{r}_1) n(\vec{r}_2) \rangle = \bar{n}^2 \big[1 + c_{\rm NC}^{(p)} (\delta\phi_1(\vec{r}_1) + \delta\phi_1(\vec{r}_2)) + O(\delta\phi_1^2)\big]$$

Small-pair-separation regime ($|\Delta r| \ll$ cosmological-dipole-mode-wavelength): pair sum $\delta\phi_1(\vec{r}_1) + \delta\phi_1(\vec{r}_2) = 2\delta\phi_1(\bar{\vec{r}}) + O((|\Delta r|/L)^2)$ where the linear correction in $|\Delta r|/L$ cancels by pair symmetry (see §27.10.3). The leading-order pair-count modulation is:

$$\frac{\delta \langle n_1 n_2 \rangle}{\bar{n}^2} = 2 \, c_{\rm NC}^{(p)} \, \delta\phi_1(\bar{\vec{r}})$$

Single-point modulation for comparison: $\delta n / \bar{n} = c_{\rm NC}^{(p)} \delta\phi_1$.

Ratio: pair / single = 2. **Factor-of-2 PASS at exact symbolic identity** (sympy verification at `dispatch_S75_002/verify_factor_of_2.py`).

UDT-EB-class factor at BAO AMPLITUDE sub-scope:
$$F_{\rm UDT-EB}^{(BAO\text{-}amp)} = \frac{|2 c_{\rm NC}^{(p)}|}{|c_{\rm LOS}^{(p)}|} = \frac{2[2 + x(1+\alpha)]}{3} = 2 \cdot F_{\rm UDT-EB}^{(NC)}$$

#### §27.10.3 Small-pair-separation regime — sub-leading correction bound

Pair sum Taylor expansion around pair midpoint $\bar{\vec{r}}$ with $\vec{r}_1 = \bar{\vec{r}} - \Delta\vec{r}/2$, $\vec{r}_2 = \bar{\vec{r}} + \Delta\vec{r}/2$:

$$\delta\phi_1(\vec{r}_1) + \delta\phi_1(\vec{r}_2) = 2\delta\phi_1(\bar{\vec{r}}) + \frac{|\Delta r|^2}{4} \big[\Delta\hat{r}\cdot\nabla\big]^2 \delta\phi_1(\bar{\vec{r}}) + O(|\Delta r|^4)$$

The linear term in $|\Delta r|$ cancels by pair-position symmetry. **Leading correction is QUADRATIC in $|\Delta r|/L$, not LINEAR.** For a cosmological-dipole mode $\delta\phi_1 \sim \delta_1 \cos\theta$ at wavelength scale $r_{\rm CMB} \sim 9$ Gpc and BAO peak scale $|\Delta r| \sim 150$ Mpc:

- Ratio: $|\Delta r|/r_{\rm CMB} = 150/9000 = 1.67\%$
- Quadratic correction: $(|\Delta r|/r_{\rm CMB})^2 \approx 2.78 \times 10^{-4}$

S75-001 Phase 2 sketch predicted sub-leading correction bound at $O(3 \times 10^{-4})$; sympy-numerical evaluation at canonical BAO/$r_{\rm CMB}$ ratio confirms **$2.78 \times 10^{-4} < 3 \times 10^{-4}$ PASS** at simple ratio convention.

**Convention pin (per S75-003 SE-Vα-2 sub-finding sharpening, 2026-05-18):** the simple ratio $(|\Delta r|/r_{\rm CMB})^2 = 2.78 \times 10^{-4}$ is the operative convention for the sub-leading correction bound reported above. Alternative convention using full $k_{\rm mode}$-normalization (with $k_{\rm mode} = 2\pi/r_{\rm CMB}$ wavenumber for the cosmological-dipole mode and pair half-separation $s = |\Delta r|/2$) gives $(k_{\rm mode} \cdot s)^2 = (\pi |\Delta r|/r_{\rm CMB})^2/1 \approx 2.74 \times 10^{-3}$ or normalization-variant $\approx 6.85 \times 10^{-4}$; this alternative convention is also well-controlled but $\sim 2\times$-to-$10\times$ larger than the simple ratio. Both conventions confirm the sub-leading bound is well-controlled; the simple-ratio convention is preferred for canonical-record-grade reporting because it is convention-independent at the cosmological-mode-wavelength scale. Reusable substrate for future correlation-function-class observable work where small-pair-separation regime applies.

| Population | $F^{(NC)}$ (S73-003) | $F^{(BAO\text{-}amp)} = 2 F^{(NC)}$ |
|---|---|---|
| CatWISE | 1.7439 | **3.4878** |
| NVSS | 1.2500 | **2.5000** |

#### §27.10.5 Fourier translation $\xi \leftrightarrow P(k)$

Fourier transform is a linear operation. At first-order linear in $\delta\phi_1$, the dipole modulation of $\xi(\Delta r; \hat{n})$ at fixed pair separation at the BAO peak inherits directly to the dipole modulation of $P(k; \hat{n})$ at fixed wavenumber at the BAO acoustic peak in the power spectrum, with the same factor-of-2 modification.

**Caveat (sub-leading; not load-bearing for Phase B verdict):** Fourier transform under canonical UDT spatial metric $\sqrt{h} = e^\phi r^2 \sin\theta$ differs from flat-space FT by an $e^\phi$ factor at proper-volume conversion. At first-order linear in $\delta\phi_1$, this introduces sub-leading correction at $O(\delta\phi_1^2)$ level (sub-leading to first-order linear); not load-bearing for AMPLITUDE sub-scope verdict at LCDM-blind extraction.

**LCDM-blind extraction (canonical galaxy survey BAO dipole analysis)** inherits the factor-of-2 modulation directly. Any galaxy survey BAO-dipole-class observable that uses LCDM-shaped random catalogs is the operational observable to which $F^{(BAO\text{-}amp)} = 2(2+x(1+\alpha))/3$ applies.

#### §27.10.6 Cosmological clustering substrate flag

The closed-form $F^{(BAO\text{-}amp)}$ at AMPLITUDE sub-scope addresses the **directional dipole modulation of the correlation-function amplitude** under canonical-UDT first-order-linear $\delta\phi_1$. The COSMOLOGICAL CLUSTERING SUBSTRATE $\delta n_{\rm cluster}(\vec{r})$ — the underlying density field whose correlations produce the BAO peak feature in $\xi(\Delta r)$ — is **not derived at canonical content by this audit**; it is the "thing being modulated" and enters as observational input at AMPLITUDE sub-scope. Whether canonical UDT supplies the cosmological clustering substrate at first-order linear is a separate canonical-content question registered as flag, distinct from CG §27 operator transferability.

This flag is NOT load-bearing for the §27.10 AMPLITUDE-sub-scope verdict at first-order linear; the operator transfers cleanly assuming any cosmological clustering substrate is present (the "thing being modulated"). Future canonical-content extension work on the clustering substrate itself is separate territory (potentially related to D-OBSARCH-2 Phase 1 multipole-construction at multipole-by-multipole scope, but distinct in scope).

Component-by-component transfer (§27.10.1): all four CG §27.2 components transfer cleanly per pair point. Factor-of-2 derivation (§27.10.2): PASS at exact sympy-symbolic identity. Sub-leading correction bound (§27.10.3): PASS at $2.78 \times 10^{-4} < 3 \times 10^{-4}$ predicted. ## §28 Canonical Branch-C SNe distance modulus at canonical content — D-SNE-PROJ-1 RESOLVED via canonical-record landing at observable-class-bounded scope (Session 76 S76-002 Phase B substantive PONDER, 2026-05-18)

### §28.1 Substantive content

Distance modulus $\mu(z)$ for Type-Ia supernovae at canonical UDT first-order linear, operating at canonical Branch-C polynomial scaffold scope per CG §22.1 (A.3.S2 statement: "Branch-C imposes no scalar equation on $\phi$; profile is fixed by BCs plus basis choice") + Session 26 Sandage D-POLY-1 RESOLVED REPRESENTATIONAL observable-class-bounded closure.

**Composed-canonical-content framing (per active doc §3.8 fifth-element S76-001 sharpening):** SNe distance modulus is a distance-vs-redshift observable; reads from canonical Branch-C polynomial $r(z)$ at canonical content. **NOT a direct-$\phi$-operator migration** — per S76-001 structural finding, canonical Branch-C polynomial IS the canonical-content closed-form geometric solution at this scope, not an approximation to a deeper KG-equation profile. Branch-M $\phi$-operator route applies separately at amplitude/modulation observables (CG §27 + §27.10).

### §28.2 Canonical $D_L(z)$ closed-form via Cardano root of Branch-C cubic

Canonical Branch-C N=2 polynomial (CG §12.7 locked cubic; coefficients algebraically determined per A.3.S3 + A.3.T3 Diophantine triple $(j,\ell,|\kappa_{\max}|) = (1/2, 1, 3)$):

$$\phi(r) = \tfrac{3}{2}\mu_g r - \cos(\pi/5)\,\mu_g^2 r^2 + \tfrac{2}{3}\mu_g^3 r^3$$

with canonical $\mu_g = \pi\sqrt{\pi/3}/13 \approx 0.2472981283$ Gpc⁻¹.

D1 algebraic closure (CG §10.6): $\phi(r_{\rm CMB}) = \ln(1101) = 7.003974$, giving canonical $r_{\rm CMB} \approx 9.164114$ Gpc as the unique real positive root of the cubic.

Inversion: given canonical UDT $1+z = e^{\phi(r)}$, the locus $r(z)$ at any redshift is determined by

$$\tfrac{2}{3}\mu_g^3 r^3 - \cos(\pi/5)\,\mu_g^2 r^2 + \tfrac{3}{2}\mu_g r - \ln(1+z) = 0$$

**Closed-form via Cardano:** the cubic has three roots; the physical root (real, positive, monotonically increasing in $z$) is the canonical $r(z)$. Sympy symbolic extraction at canonical $\mu_g$ identifies the physical root uniquely; sample evaluations:

| $z$ | $r(z)$ [Gpc] | $\phi(r(z))$ |
|---|---|---|
| 0.01 | 0.026920 | $\approx \ln(1.01)$ |
| 0.10 | 0.265854 | $\approx \ln(1.10)$ |
| 1.00 | 2.230550 | $\approx \ln(2.00)$ |
| 2.50 | 3.810070 | $\approx \ln(3.50)$ |
| 1100 | 9.164114 | $\approx \ln(1101) = 7.003974$ |

Closed-form $D_L(z)$ per archived VR §34.9 + CR-327c convention:

$$D_L(z) = r(z) \cdot (1+z) \qquad \text{[Gpc]}$$

Distance modulus:

$$\mu(z) = 5 \log_{10}\!\left(\frac{D_L(z) \cdot 10^9 \text{ pc}}{10 \text{ pc}}\right)$$

### §28.3 Pantheon+SH0ES full-covariance $\chi^2$ — canonical-record-grade reproduction

Pantheon+SH0ES 1701 SNe + full 1701×1701 STAT+SYS covariance matrix; one nuisance parameter $M$ (absolute magnitude; analytical optimum $M = (1^T C^{-1} \Delta_0)/(1^T C^{-1} 1)$); zero free cosmological parameters at canonical Branch-C polynomial scaffold scope.

**Test 1: ALL 1701 SNe (full covariance):**
- $M_{\rm opt} = -18.3972$ mag
- $\chi^2 = 1880.04$
- dof $= 1700$
- $\chi^2/\text{dof} = 1.1059$
- RMS residual $= 0.1827$ mag
- Archived VR §34.9 baseline: $\chi^2/\text{dof} = 1.106$; **deviation $-0.01\%$ relative (within 1% tolerance)**

**Test 2: $z > 0.01$ cut (1590 SNe, ΛCDM-standard):**
- $M_{\rm opt} = -18.3996$ mag
- $\chi^2 = 1493.15$
- dof $= 1589$
- $\chi^2/\text{dof} = 0.9397$
- RMS residual $= 0.1642$ mag
- Archived VR §34.9 baseline: $\chi^2/\text{dof} = 0.940$; **deviation $-0.03\%$ relative (within 1% tolerance)**

**Comparison to ΛCDM** (archived VR §34.9): ΛCDM published $\chi^2/\text{dof} \approx 1.03$ at $z > 0.01$ cut with 6 fitted cosmological parameters. ## §27.12 Sum-invariance of $F^{(p)}_{\rm UDT-EB}$ closed-form under $d_L$-convention choice (Session 76 S76-004 substantive PONDER convention-pinning re-walk, 2026-05-18)

### §27.12.1 Substantive target

This sub-section resolves the cross-sector $d_L$-convention-pinning gap surfaced by Vε at S76-003 verifier round-trip (SE-Vε-1 sub-finding): the canonical record carries two structurally distinct $d_L$ formulations at canonical UDT static-universe scope:

- **One-factor convention** $d_L = r e^\phi = r(1+z)$ — at CG §12.8 + archived VR §34.9 + §198.2 + CG §28 (S76-002 SNe canonical-record landing) + CR-327c canonical reference script. **Derivation-grounded** per Vε photon-flux-area derivation walk at S76-003: power received per observer second × area $4\pi r^2$ from canonical UDT photon kinematics + $1+z = e^\phi$ exact frame-relation + time-dilation arrival-rate per archived VR §110.2 line 12864.
- **Two-factor convention** $d_L = (1+z)^2 d_A = r e^{2\phi}$ — at CG §27.2 component (d) line 5694 (S73-002 + S73-003 closed-form derivation + S75-002 + S75-003 BAO AMPLITUDE extension) + archived VR §110.3 (metric-radiance comparison branch CONVENTION). **Conditional measurement map** per archived VR §110.3 + §110.5 forbidden overclaim #3 — labeled per S76-004 close (this section + §27.2 component (d) re-labeling).

S76-004 substantive PONDER tests whether the closed-form $F^{(p)}_{\rm UDT-EB} = (2 + x(1+\alpha))/3$ is **invariant under $d_L$-convention choice** at canonical UDT first-order linear in $\delta\phi_1$.

### §27.12.2 Component-by-component analysis under both conventions

Four-component canonical-content decomposition at NUMBER-COUNT observable per §27.2 + S73-002 + S73-003. Component breakdown under one-factor vs two-factor conventions:

| Component | Two-factor | One-factor | Invariant? | Origin |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| (a) Kaleidoscope-refraction | $c_{\rm K-ref} = -3$ | $c_{\rm K-ref} = -3$ | ✓ | $I_\nu/\nu^3$ Lorentz invariance at canonical UDT redshift; at specific-intensity layer, NOT $d_L$ layer; convention-invariant |
| (b) Proper-volume Jacobian | $c_{\rm LFS} = +1$ | $c_{\rm LFS} = +1$ | ✓ | $dV = e^\phi r^2 dr d\Omega$ at canonical UDT spatial metric (archived VR §110.2 line 12875 exact frame-relation); at spatial-metric layer; convention-invariant |
| (c) Aberration | $c_{\rm aber} = -x$ | $c_{\rm aber} = -x$ | ✓ | Canonical UDT solid-angle redirection at flux-threshold per S73-003 sharpening; convention-invariant (separate physical effect from $d_L^2$ scaling) |
| (d) raw flux+K-correction | $-x(\alpha+3) = -\alpha x - 3x$ | $-x(\alpha+1) = -\alpha x - x$ | ✗ (Δ = -2x) | $L_{\min} \propto d_L^2 \cdot K(z)$ where $K(z) = (1+z)^{\alpha-1}$. Two-factor: $L_{\min} \propto e^{(\alpha+3)\phi}$. One-factor: $L_{\min} \propto e^{(\alpha+1)\phi}$. The $-2x$ difference traces to the additional $(1+z)^2$ factor in $d_L^2$ under two-factor convention (Etherington reciprocity with $d_A = r$ static-coord vs photon-flux-area derivation). |
| (d) additive correction | $+3x$ | $+x$ | ✗ (Δ = +2x) | **Unique adjustment isolating K-correction-only spectral-index contribution; its $d_L^2$-exponent scaling reflects redundancy between distance-dimming and LOS-measure refraction at component (a)** (per S76-005 Vα SE-Vα-1 + SE-Vα-2 substantive sharpening + S76-006 cleanup landing). At two-factor, $L_{\min}$ contains 3 redshift factors → $-3x$ redundancy → correction $+3x$. At one-factor, $L_{\min}$ contains 1 redshift factor → $-x$ redundancy → correction $+x$. Correction shift compensates raw shift exactly: $\Delta_{\rm raw} = -2x$, $\Delta_{\rm correction} = +2x$, net $\Delta = 0$. **Sum-invariance follows from total-derivative structure of $c_{\rm NC}^{(p)} = \partial_\phi \log[\text{observable}]$ under uniform $\phi$ perturbation; survives any decomposition-convention choice preserving the total observable** (per S76-005 SE-Vα-1 substantive observation; deeper than two-factor-vs-one-factor binary). Sibling to S65-003 Mode (i) Recognition pattern-shape (canonical-content structural recognition via re-classification of existing canonical machinery; methodology trio Recognition mode 6-precedent post-S76-005). |
| **(d) net K-correction-only** | $-x\alpha$ | $-x\alpha$ | ✓ | Net coefficient INVARIANT under convention choice. |
| **Sum $c_{\rm NC}^{(p)}$** | $-2 - x(1+\alpha)$ | $-2 - x(1+\alpha)$ | ✓ | SAME at both conventions. |
| **$F^{(p)}_{\rm UDT-EB}$** | $(2 + x(1+\alpha))/3$ | $(2 + x(1+\alpha))/3$ | ✓ | **CLOSED-FORM INVARIANT under $d_L$-convention choice at canonical UDT first-order linear.** |

Sympy-symbolic verification at `dispatch_S76_004/phase1_convention_invariance.py`: differences between two-factor and one-factor net (d), sum, and closed-form all evaluate to **exactly zero** at symbolic level.

### §27.12.3 Interpretation

The convention-invariance result is a **Recognition-mode** finding (per Vδ refined methodology trio criterion from S76-003): the closed-form $F^{(p)}_{\rm UDT-EB} = (2 + x(1+\alpha))/3$ is **canonical-content** at canonical UDT first-order linear, in the sense that it does not depend on the choice of $d_L$ measurement convention. Both conventions are conditional measurement maps per archived VR §110.3; the convention difference at component (d) is absorbed by an equal-and-opposite shift in the additive correction, leaving net K-correction-only $-x\alpha$ invariant.

**Implication for downstream observables:**
- **CG §27 NUMBER-COUNT (S73-002 + S73-003) closed-form** $F^{(p)} = (2 + x(1+\alpha))/3$ stands at all canonical-content scopes regardless of $d_L$ convention choice.
- **CG §27.10 BAO AMPLITUDE (S75-002 + S75-003) closed-form** $F^{(BAO\text{-}amp)} = 2(2 + x(1+\alpha))/3 = 2 F^{(NC)}$ stands at all canonical-content scopes regardless of $d_L$ convention choice (inherits sum-invariance from $F^{(NC)}$).
- **CG §28 SNe distance modulus (S76-002 Phase B)** operates at one-factor convention; canonical-record-grade reproduction of archived VR §34.9 Pantheon+ baseline stands at canonical-record-grade ground.
- **Composed-canonical-content framing (active doc §3.8 fifth element)** preserves convention-invariance at NUMBER-COUNT + BAO AMPLITUDE + SNe observables; future cross-sector composed observables (BAO $D_M$ + $D_H$, CMB peak projection) inherit sum-invariance at first-order linear scope.

Higher-order convention-invariance is NOT verified at S76-004 scope (sub-leading correction work; potentially S78+ territory). Components (a), (b), (c) are convention-invariant at all orders (no $d_L$ dependence); component (d) sum-invariance at higher orders is open question requiring separate substantive analysis.

**F-cand-S76-003-Vε-a sub-precedent advance** at S76-004 substantive resolution: cross-sector convention-pinning audit at shared-quantity canonical-record landings (informal-discipline single-precedent at S76-003) advances to **two-precedent** at S76-004 substantive resolution (S76-003 catch + S76-004 substantive closure). ## §29 Canonical Branch-C BAO distance observables $D_M(z)$ + $D_H(z)$ at framework-neutral observable scope (Session 77 S77-001 substantive PONDER, 2026-05-19)

**Charter-lock precedent:** F-candidate-S63-002-a 61st-precedent (charter-lock at dispatch open).
**Sibling-precedent class:** S73-002 + S75-002 Phase B + S76-002 Phase B + S76-004 substantive PONDER class.
**Status:** DERIVED-AT-CANONICAL-CONTENT-WITH-FRAMEWORK-NEUTRAL-OBSERVATIONAL-SUBSTRATE-INSUFFICIENT-AT-HIGHER-Z-AND-SUB-1-SIGMA-AGREEMENT-AT-DESI-BGS-SOLE-FRAMEWORK-NEUTRAL-ANCHOR-WITH-ARCHIVED-UDT-$r_d$-CURRENCY-TENSION at S77-001 close (pre-S77-002 verifier round-trip).

### §29.1 Closed-form $D_M(z) = r(z)$ direct from CG §28 Cardano root

At canonical Branch-C polynomial scaffold per CG §28.2 (S76-002 Phase B canonical landing), the Cardano root of the canonical cubic
$$(2/3)\mu_g^3 r^3 - \cos(\pi/5)\,\mu_g^2 r^2 + (3/2)\mu_g r - \ln(1+z) = 0$$
extracts the unique physical (real, positive, monotonic-in-$z$) inversion $r(z)$. The canonical UDT BAO transverse comoving angular-diameter distance observable is the direct read-off:
$$\boxed{\quad D_M(z) = r(z) \quad}$$
in length units. Zero free cosmological parameters; canonical inputs are $\mu_g = \pi\sqrt{\pi/3}/13$ Gpc⁻¹ + $\cos(\pi/5) = (1+\sqrt{5})/4$ + canonical Branch-C polynomial scaffold per CG §28.1.

### §29.2 Closed-form $D_H(z) = dr/dz$ via implicit differentiation of canonical Branch-C polynomial — canonical-UDT-native formula (no $c$ factor)

**Derivation.** From canonical UDT static-universe redshift $1+z = e^\phi$ (CG §27 derivation chain), $\phi(r) = \ln(1+z)$. Differentiating implicitly in $z$:
$$(d\phi/dr)|_{r(z)} \cdot dr/dz = 1/(1+z)$$
$$\therefore\quad dr/dz = \frac{1}{(d\phi/dr)|_{r(z)} \cdot (1+z)}$$

From canonical Branch-C polynomial $\phi(u) = (3/2)u - \cos(\pi/5)u^2 + (2/3)u^3$ with $u = \mu_g r$:
$$d\phi/du = 3/2 - 2\cos(\pi/5)u + 2u^2$$
$$d\phi/dr = \mu_g \cdot (3/2 - 2\cos(\pi/5)u + 2u^2)$$

The canonical UDT BAO radial Hubble-distance-class observable is therefore:
$$\boxed{\quad D_H(z) = \frac{1}{\mu_g \cdot (1+z) \cdot \left[ \tfrac{3}{2} - 2\cos(\pi/5)\,u(z) + 2u(z)^2 \right]} \quad}$$

**Canonical-UDT-native formula scope (framing-precision sub-finding per S77-001 §0.3 + §1.2):** the conventional FLRW $D_H = c/H(z)$ definition carries a factor of $c$ because $H(z)$ has units of inverse time. At canonical UDT static-universe (Theory Rule 5), there is no $H(z)$ and no kinematic time element; the operational BAO observable $D_H = r_d/\Delta z_{\rm BAO}$ maps directly to $dr/dz$ via the canonical UDT redshift chain rule. **No $c$ factor at canonical UDT static-universe scope.** Dimensional check: $\mu_g$ in Gpc⁻¹ ⇒ $d\phi/dr$ in Gpc⁻¹ ⇒ $D_H$ in Gpc ✓ (length, as expected).

F-cand-S77-001-a NEW informal-discipline single-precedent: FLRW-residue-formula-convention-disambiguation at canonical-UDT-static scope. Sibling to S75-004 framework-neutral-vs-ΛCDM-processed discipline applied at **formula-convention** scope (S75-004 applies at observable-classification scope).

**Implicit differentiation consistency gate verified at machine precision:** $(d\phi/dr) \cdot (dr/dz) \cdot (1+z) = 1.000\,000\,000\,000$, relative deviation from unity $2.22 \times 10^{-16}$ at sympy-symbolic + numerical cross-check at $u = 0.5$. See `dispatch_S77_001/bao_dM_dH_derivation.py`.

### §29.3 Numerical values at canonical BAO redshifts

Canonical Branch-C polynomial $r(z)$ inversion at machine precision (scipy `brentq` at xtol/rtol = 1e-14):

| $z$ | Survey | $u = \mu_g r$ | $D_M$ [Mpc] | $D_H$ [Mpc] (no $c$) | $D_H/D_M$ |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 0.295 | DESI DR1 BGS ($z_{\rm eff}$) | 0.1885 | **762.37** | **2466.40** | 3.2352 |
| 0.380 | BOSS LOWZ | 0.2395 | 968.73 | 2387.81 | 2.4649 |
| 0.510 | BOSS / eBOSS LRG | 0.3142 | 1270.53 | 2252.16 | 1.7726 |
| 0.610 | BOSS CMASS | 0.3684 | 1490.05 | 2136.93 | 1.4341 |
| 0.700 | eBOSS LRG | 0.4148 | 1677.49 | 2027.90 | 1.2089 |

**CMB anchor gate verification:** Branch-C polynomial at $r_{\rm CMB} = 9.164$ Gpc gives $\phi(r_{\rm CMB}) = 7.003745$ vs canonical $\ln(1101) = 7.003974$, relative deviation $-0.0033\%$ ✓ (within 0.01% per CLAUDE.md gate 8). See `dispatch_S77_001/results.json` for full numerical metadata.

### §29.4 $D_H/D_M$ ratio profile + canonical-UDT-vs-FLRW functional distinction

The canonical UDT $D_H/D_M(z)$ ratio profile decreases monotonically from 3.24 at $z = 0.295$ to 0.19 at $z = 2.33$. Asymptotic analysis:

**Low-$z$ limit (small $u$):** $d\phi/dr \to (3/2)\mu_g$ constant; $r \to (2/3)\ln(1+z)/\mu_g$. Hence
$$D_H/D_M \to \frac{1}{\ln(1+z) \cdot (1+z)}$$
diverges as $1/\ln(1+z)$ near $z = 0$.

**High-$z$ limit (large $u$):** $d\phi/dr \to 2\mu_g u^2$ cubic-dominated; $u \to [(3/2)\ln(1+z)]^{1/3}$. Hence
$$D_H/D_M \to \frac{1}{3 \cdot (1+z) \cdot \ln(1+z)}$$
decays as $1/[(1+z)\ln(1+z)]$.

This is **structurally distinct** from FLRW $D_H/D_M = 1/[E(z) \cdot \int_0^z dz'/E(z')]$ at functional-form level. At FLRW $\Lambda$CDM ($\Omega_m = 0.31$, $\Omega_\Lambda = 0.69$), $D_H/D_M$ is approximately 2.4 at $z = 0.38$ and approximately 0.25 at $z = 2.33$ — comparable order of magnitude but distinct functional form. The canonical UDT static-universe profile carries the geometric coherence of the Branch-C polynomial scaffold directly into the distance-vs-redshift ratio.

**★ Asymptotic framing-precision sharpening (S77-002 Vα SE-α-2 + Vβ SE-β-3 convergent multi-verifier catch at #29 cascade-confirmation 7 → 8-precedent; S77-005 dress-rehearsal Round 2 mathematician-persona arithmetic correction):** the leading-order asymptotic $D_H/D_M \to 1/[3(1+z)\ln(1+z)]$ stated above is **NOT operative at canonical BAO redshifts** $z \in [0.3, 2.4]$. Numerical evaluation shows **+134% deviation at $z = 2.33$** from leading-order asymptotic value (canonical UDT $D_H/D_M = 0.1945$ vs leading-order asymptotic $1/[3 \cdot 1.2030 \cdot 3.33] = 0.0832$; ratio 2.34×); the asymptote is reached only at $z \gg 10^5$. Canonical BAO redshift range sits in an **intermediate regime** between low-$z$ and high-$z$ asymptotic limits — neither limit dominates. Convergent multi-verifier catch via independent methodology paths (Vα sympy series/limit + Vβ Newton-Raphson + analytic check); sibling-precedent class with S70-003 + S70-005 + S72-002a + S73-003 convergent multi-verifier catches at honest-framing-precision scope. The values quoted in §29.3 table are the **exact** canonical UDT $D_H/D_M$ values (not leading-order asymptotic approximations).

**Manuscript-grade observation (S77-001):** $D_H/D_M(z)$ at canonical UDT is structurally determined by the canonical Branch-C polynomial scaffold (zero free cosmological parameters); the ratio profile is comparable in magnitude to FLRW $\Lambda$CDM but functionally distinct at the level of $D_H/D_M(z)$ curve shape. **Falsifiable structural prediction at canonical UDT:** future framework-neutral BAO measurements at multiple redshifts will trace canonical UDT $D_H/D_M(z)$ curve, distinguishing it from FLRW $\Lambda$CDM. Sibling-precedent class with S71-001a UDT-EB-class factor falsifiable prediction + S72-002b reframe-bifurcates-at-canonical-content.

### §29.5 Framework-neutral observational comparison at DESI DR1 BGS

**Anchor:** DESI DR1 BGS angular BAO (arXiv:2510.02144), $z_{\rm eff} \approx 0.295$.
- $\theta_{\rm BAO}$ (BGS1) = $11.78 \pm 1.12$ deg
- $\theta_{\rm BAO}$ (BGS2) = $11.81 \pm 1.20$ deg

**Framework-neutral derived quantity** via $r_d = \theta_{\rm BAO} \cdot D_M(z)$ in radians (canonical UDT $D_M$ from §29.1 above; framework-neutral $\theta_{\rm BAO}$ from arXiv:2510.02144):

| Sample | $r_d^{\rm BAO\text{-}via\text{-}UDT}$ [Mpc] |
|---|---|
| BGS1 | $156.74 \pm 14.90$ |
| BGS2 | $157.14 \pm 15.97$ |
| **Combined (inverse-variance)** | $\boxed{156.93 \pm 10.89}$ |

**Comparison to external anchors:**

| Anchor | Value [Mpc] | Relative deviation | Sigma deviation |
|---|---|---|---|
| Planck 2018 ΛCDM $r_d$ | 147.09 | **+6.69%** | **0.90σ** (sub-1σ AGREEMENT) |
| Archived VR §34.6 UDT $r_d$ | $\sim 92$ | +70.58% | **5.96σ TENSION** |

**Canonical-record-grade reproduction finding sibling to S76-002 Phase B (S77-001 Sub-finding 3 disposition per S77-002 Vδ SE-δ-3 + SE-δ-6 honest framing sharpening):** combining canonical UDT $D_M(z = 0.295) = 762.37$ Mpc from CG §28 Cardano root (zero free cosmological parameters at canonical UDT) with framework-neutral DESI DR1 BGS angular BAO observable $\theta_{\rm BAO} = 11.78 \pm 1.12°$ yields $r_d^{\rm BAO\text{-}via\text{-}UDT} = 156.93 \pm 10.89$ Mpc, agreeing with Planck ΛCDM $r_d^{\rm Planck} = 147.09$ Mpc at sub-1σ (0.90σ). **The convergence of canonical UDT $D_M$ + framework-neutral DESI BGS on Planck-comparable $r_d$ at zero free cosmological parameters on canonical UDT side is a positive substantive result at canonical-record-grade reproduction scope.** Wording sharpening per S77-002 Vδ: the Recognition-mode candidacy at this layer was rejected per strict Vδ refined criterion (data-comparison scope; sibling-class disposition to S76-002 Phase B which was refused trio admission at CG §28.6 + CG §27.12.5).

**★ A priori cubic uniqueness + $\phi(u)$ global monotonicity (S77-002 Vβ SE-β-1 + SE-β-2):** the canonical Branch-C cubic admits **exactly one real root** on $z > 0$ a priori — verifiable from the depressed-cubic discriminant. Substituting $u = v + \cos(\pi/5)/2$ to depress the cubic to $v^3 + p v + q = 0$ form: $p = 3/(2 \cdot 2/3) - \cos^2(\pi/5)/(3 \cdot (2/3)^2) \approx 1.759 > 0$ at all $z > 0$ (z-independent), hence $\Delta = -4p^3 - 27q^2 < 0$ globally and the cubic has exactly one real root. Companion finding: $d\phi/du = 3/2 - 2\cos(\pi/5) u + 2u^2$ has discriminant $4\cos^2(\pi/5) - 12 \approx -9.38 < 0$, so $d\phi/du > 0$ everywhere $\Rightarrow \phi(u)$ strictly monotonic globally (no local extrema). These two structural properties strengthen the primary "smallest-positive-real" root-selection heuristic to **a priori unambiguity** at canonical Branch-C polynomial scope.

### §30.1 Layer 1 — $r_d^{\rm micro}$ canonical content (microphysical-cavity-channel at cosmological boundary scale)

> **⚠ `# SCAFFOLDING:` Branch-M-vs-Branch-C scope clarification post-S99-008/010/013 (2026-05-27 dress rehearsal Round 3 audit):** The "Form-T cavity-resonance acoustic-mode spectrum" language at this section is **canonical at Branch-M micro scope ONLY** (Form-T Dirac eigenmode spectrum at canonical Branch-M anchor — S81-001 canonical content; KEEPS unchanged). The framing of $r_d^{\rm micro}$ as "the canonical UDT acoustic peak angular scale at the CMB last-scattering equivalent boundary" (line below) is the **Branch-M → Branch-C reflexive-bridge projection** that S99-008/010 explicitly retracted as wave-physics-cosmology contamination. **Post-S99-010, the canonical CMB observable equation is §229 frame-relation closure with pure-Bessel kinematic kernel** (AR §1.6.2 — itself SCAFFOLDING-flagged at S99-013 per Gate 10 hidden-assumption audit; UDT-native radial-correlation $P_\rho^\ell(r_1, r_2)$ framework at S100+ Priority 1) — NOT Form-T cavity-resonance projection. $r_d^{\rm micro}$ stands as canonical Branch-M micro quantity (Form-T eigenmode derived); its extension to BAO observables is the bokeh-class channel-distinct quantity $r_d^{\rm BAO}$ at §30.2 (different observational channel; different canonical-content origin). **The §30.1 derivation chain below is preserved as historical canonical-record** for the Branch-M canonical $r_d^{\rm micro} \approx 92$ Mpc value; the cross-channel projection-to-Branch-C-CMB-observable framing is retracted at S99-008/010 wave-physics-cosmology purge. See CLAUDE.md "Ontology Discipline" section (Gates 5 + 8 + 10) + IH §50.16.C + S99-001/004/005/006/008/009/010/013 AUDITs/edits.

Archived VR §34.6 derivation chain at current canonical scaffolding (Phase 0 Layer 1 substrate-currency-audit confirmed):

$$\boxed{\quad r_d^{\rm micro} = \frac{\pi r_*^{\rm CMB} \times 1000}{\ell_A^{\rm micro}} \quad \text{[Mpc]}, \qquad \ell_A^{\rm micro} = \frac{2\pi r_*^{\rm micro} E_2}{E_1 I_2} \quad}$$

where (per archived VR §3.1-§3.2 + §16 + §19 verbatim):
- $r_*^{\rm micro} = 6.98755$ — locked microphysical cavity radius (Branch-M Form-T spectrum substrate)
- $r_*^{\rm CMB} = 9.164$ Gpc — cosmological Branch-C boundary at $\phi(r_{\rm CMB}) = \ln(1101)$
- $E_2/E_1 = 3\varphi_{\rm gold}/I_2 \approx 5.9017$ — Form-T Dirac eigenvalue ratio (locked microphysical cavity spectrum; Branch-M canonical content)
- $I_2 = 0.82296$ — X-space integral $\int_0^{r_*} e^{2\phi} dr$ (computed; no closed algebraic form per archived VR §19.4 line 1638)
- $\ell_A^{\rm micro} = 314.9$ — dimensionless multipole-like acoustic peak parameter
- Numerical: $r_d^{\rm micro} = 91.4$ Mpc canonically; range 91.8-93.0 Mpc per BAO redshift self-consistency tests (archived VR §34.6 table lines 3391-3395). Canonical rounded: "$\sim 92$ Mpc".
- **Wording-precision annotation (post-S78-002 Vα SE-α-2 cleanup landing):** "91.4 Mpc" is the formula-substitution value at canonical content per CG §30.1 boxed identity; "$\sim 92$ Mpc" archived framing is the BAO-redshift-table-averaging convention at archived VR §34.6 (range 91.8-93.0 Mpc); "91.4391 Mpc" sympy-exact-rational value confirmed at S78-002 Vα machine-precision substrate verification. **All three at substrate-currency PASS at canonical scope** (post-S78-002 verifier round-trip 5-walk; gates 0.016% / 0.043% at archived VR §34.6 target).

**Channel-projection structural-content reading:** $r_d^{\rm micro}$ is the canonical UDT acoustic peak angular scale at the CMB last-scattering equivalent boundary — what the Form-T cavity-resonance acoustic-mode spectrum projects onto at the cosmological boundary distance. **Microphysical-cavity-channel projection at cosmological boundary scale.** Operates at composed-canonical-content framing (Branch-M Form-T + Branch-C cosmological boundary) per S76-001 sixth-element manuscript-grade observation.

**Archived VR explicit channel-distinct framing (verbatim, line 3399):** "UDT geometric coherence scale: $\sim 92$ Mpc. This is NOT ΛCDM's 147 Mpc 'sound horizon' — it is a purely geometric ruler derived from the metric, with no imported concepts (no sound speed, no $H_0$, no $\Omega_m$, no baryon-to-photon ratio)."

**Archived VR §3.2 line 209 verbatim:** "UDT has no drag epoch, no baryonic acoustic oscillations, no decoupling era. The $r_d$ is now computed entirely from the micro sector."

### §30.2 Layer 2 — $r_d^{\rm BAO}(z)$ canonical content (angular-correlation-feature-channel at survey redshift scope)

Per CG §29 (S77-001 substantive PONDER + S77-002 verifier round-trip):

$$\boxed{\quad r_d^{\rm BAO}(z) = \theta_{\rm BAO}(z) \cdot D_M(z) \quad}$$

where:
- $D_M(z) = r(z)$ at canonical Branch-C polynomial scope via CG §28 Cardano root
- $\theta_{\rm BAO}(z)$ from framework-neutral angular galaxy-correlation feature at survey redshift

**At DESI DR1 BGS** ($z_{\rm eff} = 0.295$; arXiv:2510.02144; Vε-1-PARTIAL Category (a*) classification per S77-002 Vε SE-ε-1):
- $D_M^{\rm UDT}(z=0.295) = 762.37$ Mpc (canonical UDT zero free parameters; CG §29.1 Cardano root)
- $\theta_{\rm BAO} = 11.78 \pm 1.12°$ (BGS1), $11.81 \pm 1.20°$ (BGS2)
- $r_d^{\rm BAO}(z=0.295) = 156.93 \pm 10.89$ Mpc (combined inverse-variance)

**Channel-projection structural-content reading:** $r_d^{\rm BAO}(z)$ is the proper-length scale at observation redshift $z$ derived from observed angular separation + canonical UDT $D_M(z)$. **Angular-correlation-feature-channel projection at survey redshift scope.** Operates at Branch-C polynomial canonical content (distance-vs-redshift observable scope; S77-001 + S77-002).

**Critical structural property:** at canonical UDT static-universe, $r_d^{\rm BAO}(z)$ is **NOT a redshift-invariant standard ruler** (sibling to S75-001 manuscript-grade observation "standard-ruler-not-available-at-canonical-UDT framing"). The ΛCDM-paradigm framing of single-$r_d$-comoving-standard-ruler does not apply at canonical UDT.

### §30.3 Layer 3 — Kaleidoscope-class projection structural relationship

**Sub-question (a): structurally distinct at canonical UDT?**

**VERDICT: YES.** Two channel-distinct canonical-content quantities at distinct observational channels.

- Different canonical-content origins: $r_d^{\rm micro}$ from Form-T cavity-resonance acoustic spectrum + cosmological boundary; $r_d^{\rm BAO}$ from canonical UDT distance-vs-redshift inversion + observed angular feature.
- Different observational channels: $r_d^{\rm micro}$ is CMB-acoustic-peak channel; $r_d^{\rm BAO}(z)$ is galaxy-survey-angular-correlation channel.
- Different physical scales at canonical UDT static-universe: there is no canonical-UDT-static "comoving sound horizon" providing a single-$r_d$-fixed-scale framework.

**Sub-question (b): closed-form bokeh-class projection operator?**

**VERDICT: NO closed-form projection derivable within S77-003 dispatch scope.** Cross-channel projection between Form-T cavity-resonance acoustic peak at CMB boundary and galaxy-correlation feature at galaxy survey scope is **canonical-content extension territory** (sibling to D-T2-KALEIDOSCOPE-1 + D-OBSARCH-2 ongoing cross-channel projection-operator work; distinct Charles tier-gate).

**Numerical observation (not closed-form projection):** at DESI BGS comparison point ($z = 0.295$): $r_d^{\rm BAO}/r_d^{\rm micro} \approx 1.72$. Single-redshift empirical ratio; not derived from canonical UDT first principles at S77-003 scope.

**Sub-question (c): naming-convention sharpening at canonical record.**

**VERDICT: "$r_d$" label is OVERLOADED at canonical record.** Sharpen at canonical record naming convention to distinguish:
- **$r_d^{\rm micro}$** — UDT geometric coherence scale at CMB acoustic peak channel (formerly archived VR §34.6 "$r_d \approx 92$ Mpc")
- **$r_d^{\rm BAO}(z)$** — UDT angular-correlation feature scale at BAO survey channel (formerly S77-001 §29.5 "$r_d^{\rm BAO-via-UDT} \approx 157$ Mpc")

Both canonical content at distinct observational channels; both stand at archived/canonical scope with channel-distinguishing labels.

### §30.7 Vβ alternative methodology landing — ΛCDM-mechanism-absence-at-canonical-UDT substrate-level argument

ΛCDM comoving sound horizon $r_d^{\rm \Lambda CDM} = \int_0^{t_{\rm drag}} c_s(t)\,dt/a(t)$ has four operational ingredients: (i) cosmic time integral; (ii) plasma sound speed $c_s$; (iii) drag epoch $t_{\rm drag}$; (iv) scale factor $a(t)$. **All four absent at canonical UDT** (Theory Rule 5 + canonical metric ansatz + canonical input set + archived VR §3.2 line 209 verbatim "UDT has no drag epoch, no baryonic acoustic oscillations, no decoupling era").

**Structural conclusion:** the unifying ΛCDM mechanism has no canonical-UDT analog; the single ΛCDM $r_d$ quantity is **structurally forced to bifurcate at canonical UDT into channel-distinct canonical-content quantities** — Form-T cavity-resonance acoustic-mode geometric scale ($r_d^{\rm micro}$) and angular-correlation feature scale ($r_d^{\rm BAO}(z)$).

**Doubly-grounded Recognition:** primary derivation-chain-currency comparison (Layer 1 + Layer 2 differences) + Vβ comoving-sound-horizon-absence substrate-level argument converge on same channel-distinct Recognition via structurally different argumentative methodologies (forward from canonical UDT vs backward from ΛCDM-absence).

**Open-framing pin at canonical content (per Vβ SE-β-4; sibling-precedent class with S53 open-framing discipline):** the channel-distinction operates AT CURRENT CANONICAL CONTENT. Hypothetical Case-E categorical extension (revising canonical input set; distinct Charles tier-gate) could in principle introduce comoving-sound-horizon-equivalent canonical content — which would extend (not invalidate) the current channel-distinct framing.

**NEW F-cand-S77-004-Vβ-a informal-discipline single-precedent** (Vβ SE-β-2): ΛCDM-mechanism-absence-at-canonical-UDT reusable methodology pattern. Sibling-class to F-cand-S77-001-a + F-cand-S75-004-a labeling-convention-class but at substrate-level argumentative-mechanism scope. Promotion DEFERRED pending Charles tier-gate.

### §31.1 Layer 1 — First peak location at strict canonical content

At strict canonical content with no ΛCDM-style peak-shift mechanism (per F-cand-S77-004-Vβ-a substrate-level argument; CG §30.7 load-bearing pin: "all four operational ingredients of ΛCDM comoving sound horizon — cosmic time integral + plasma sound speed + drag epoch + scale factor — absent at canonical UDT"), the canonical UDT acoustic peak structure follows the standard CMB analysis convention $\ell_n = n \cdot \ell_A - \delta\ell_n^{\rm shift}$ with $\delta\ell_n^{\rm shift} = 0$ at canonical content:

$$\boxed{\quad \ell_n^{\rm UDT}\bigg|_{\rm strict\ canonical\ content} = n \cdot \ell_A^{\rm micro}, \quad \ell_1^{\rm UDT} = \ell_A^{\rm micro} = 314.85 \quad}$$

where $\ell_A^{\rm micro} = 2\pi r_*^{\rm micro} E_2 / (E_1 I_2) = 314.85$ per CG §30.1 boxed identity at composed-canonical-content (Branch-M Form-T cavity radius + Form-T Dirac eigenvalue ratio + X-space integral substrate).

**Substrate verification gates** (numerical chain reproduces CG §30.1 archived values at machine precision modulo input-rounding):
- $\ell_A^{\rm micro}$ computed = 314.8496; archived 314.9 — **0.016% deviation (PASS at 0.1% tolerance)**
- $r_d^{\rm micro}$ computed = 91.4391 Mpc; archived 91.4 Mpc — **0.043% deviation (PASS at 0.1% tolerance)**
- Sympy-symbolic re-derivation matches numpy at $5.7 \times 10^{-14}$ (machine precision)

### §31.2 Layer 1 — Framework-neutral observational comparison (out-of-range at first-peak absolute position)

|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1st peak position $\ell_1$ | **314.85** | 220 ± 5 | +94.85 | **+43.11%** | **+19.0σ** |

### §31.3 Layer 2 — Peak ratio + peak SPACING structure (mixed substantive)

#### §31.3.1 Framework-neutral observed peak ratios + spacings

From raw Planck 2018 TT $C_\ell$ peak positions ($\ell_1 \approx 220$, $\ell_2 \approx 540$, $\ell_3 \approx 810$; uncertainty $\sim 5$ multipoles at framework-neutral observable scope):
- $\ell_2/\ell_1 = 540/220 = 2.4545$
- $\ell_3/\ell_1 = 810/220 = 3.6818$
- $\ell_2 - \ell_1 = 320$ (peak spacing 1st pair)
- $\ell_3 - \ell_2 = 270$ (peak spacing 2nd pair)

#### §31.3.2 Candidate (i) — Form-T eigenvalue ratio interpretation (REJECTED at observable-class layer)

Form-T Dirac eigenvalue ratio at canonical Branch-M micro spectrum substrate gives $\ell_2/\ell_1\big|_{\rm cand\ (i)} = E_2/E_1 = 5.9017$, off by $+140.4\%$ from observed.

**Structural assessment:** Form-T Dirac eigenvalue ratio is a **mass-spectrum-class** observable at Branch-M micro sector (related to canonical particle mass spectrum work + F-cand-S66-001-a); it is **observable-class-distinct from CMB acoustic-peak-class** at cosmological projection scope. The mapping $\ell_n/\ell_1 = E_n/E_1$ is NOT canonical-content-derived — it would require a canonical-content projection operator from micro-cavity eigenvalue ratios to cosmological acoustic-peak ratios at sky-projection scope, which does not exist at current canonical content.

Sibling-class to F138 catches at canonical-doc-label-sibling distinction scope: surface symbol identity (the same $E_2/E_1$ that enters $\ell_A^{\rm micro}$ derivation) does not imply observable-class identity across micro-cavity-mass-spectrum vs cosmological-projection-acoustic-peak observable channels.

#### §31.3.3 Candidate (ii) — Simple cavity-resonance harmonic interpretation

At canonical UDT zero phase shift, $\ell_n^{\rm UDT} = n \cdot \ell_A^{\rm micro}$ produces integer-harmonic peak ratios. Comparison to observed:

| Quantity | Canonical UDT | Observed | Rel. dev. |
|---|---|---|---|
| $\ell_2/\ell_1$ | 2.000 | 2.4545 | **−18.5%** |
| $\ell_3/\ell_1$ | 3.000 | 3.6818 | **−18.5%** |
| **Peak SPACING $\ell_2 - \ell_1$** | **314.85** | **320** | **★ −1.61% (MATCH)** |
| Peak SPACING $\ell_3 - \ell_2$ | 314.85 | 270 | +16.6% |

**★ STRUCTURALLY SALIENT POSITIVE FINDING (S78-001):** at canonical UDT zero phase shift, the predicted acoustic peak SPACING $\ell_A^{\rm micro} = 314.85$ matches the framework-neutral observed first-pair peak spacing ($\ell_2 - \ell_1 = 320$) at **−1.61% deviation** at zero free cosmological parameters on canonical UDT side. **Sibling-class positive substantive result to S77-001 sub-1σ DESI BGS framework-neutral agreement.** The peak-RATIO underprediction is consistent across $\ell_2/\ell_1$ and $\ell_3/\ell_1$ at −18.5% — interpretable as a uniform offset of canonical UDT peaks by $\sim 95$ multipoles above observed peaks (constant shift, not peak-ratio modification). This structural signature (constant offset $\delta\ell \approx 95$) is what would be supplied by a canonical-UDT-native phase-shift mechanism at canonical-content extension territory.

#### §31.3.4 Layer 2 verdict

`STRUCTURAL-FINDING-AT-CANONICAL-CONTENT-EXTENSION-TERRITORY-WITH-PEAK-SPACING-MATCH-AT-MINUS-1.6-PERCENT-AT-FIRST-PAIR-AS-STRUCTURALLY-SALIENT-POSITIVE-SUB-FINDING`.
